title: Spontaneous spiking activity of hundreds of neurons in mouse somatosensory cortex slice cultures recorded using a dense 512 electrode array
authors: Ito, S.; Yeh, F.-C.; Timme, N.M.; Hottowy, P.; Litke, A.M.; Beggs, J.M.
key_finding: CRCNS ssc-3: spontaneous multi-unit spiking from mouse S1 slices on 512-channel MEA, publicly released with spike times and metadata (DOI 10.6080/K07D2S2F)
hypothesis_generated: Use ssc-3 as ground-truth biological avalanche reference to calibrate DL activation-avalanche pipelines

title: Survey of spiking in the mouse visual system reveals functional hierarchy
authors: Siegle, J.H.; Jia, X.; Durand, S.; Gale, S.; Bennett, C.; Graddis, N.; Heller, G.; Ramirez, T.K.; Choi, H.; Luviano, J.A.; Groblewski, P.A.; Ahmed, R.; Arkhipov, A.; Bernard, A.; Billeh, Y.N.; Brown, D.; Buice, M.A.; Cain, N.; Caldejon, S.; Casal, L.; Cho, A.; Chvilicek, M.; Cox, T.C.; Dai, K.; Denman, D.J.; de Vries, S.E.J.; Dietzman, R.; Esposito, L.; Farrell, C.; Feng, D.; Galbraith, J.; Garrett, M.; Gelfand, E.C.; Hancock, N.; Harris, J.A.; Howard, R.; Hu, B.; Hytnen, R.; Iyer, R.; Jessett, E.; Johnson, K.; Kato, I.; Kiggins, J.; Lambert, S.; Lecoq, J.; Ledochowitsch, P.; Lee, J.H.; Leon, A.; Li, Y.; Liang, E.; Long, F.; Mace, K.; Melchior, J.; Millman, D.; Mollenkopf, T.; Nayan, C.; Ng, L.; Ngo, K.; Nguyen, T.; Nicovich, P.R.; North, K.; Ocker, G.K.; Ollerenshaw, D.; Oliver, M.; Pachitariu, M.; Perkins, J.; Reding, M.; Reid, D.; Robertson, M.; Ronellenfitch, K.; Seid, S.; Slaughterbeck, C.; Stoecklin, M.; Sullivan, D.; Sutton, B.; Swapp, J.; Thompson, C.; Turner, K.; Wakeman, W.; Whitesell, J.D.; Williams, D.; Williford, A.; Young, R.; Zeng, H.; Naylor, S.; Phillips, J.W.; Reid, R.C.; Mihalas, S.; Olsen, S.R.; Koch, C.
key_finding: Allen Visual Coding Neuropixels: simultaneous recordings from ~100k neurons across six cortical and two thalamic visual areas in awake mice, revealing a functional hierarchy (DOI 10.1038/s41586-020-03171-x)
hypothesis_generated: Evaluate avalanche exponents across cortical hierarchy and match to per-layer DL criticality signatures

title: Tuning Universality in Deep Neural Networks
authors: Ghavasieh, A.
key_finding: Landau + directed-percolation description of activity cascades in random DNNs shows activation-function choice selects between two universality classes (log-trap BM vs absorbed BM)
hypothesis_generated: Universality class of avalanche statistics is a tunable macroscopic knob controlled by activation nonlinearity

title: Intuition emerges in Maximum Caliber models at criticality
authors: Arola-Fernández, L.
key_finding: Maze-walking models trained with a Maximum-Caliber objective exhibit a fragile intermediate phase with hysteresis and multistability where novel goal-directed strategies appear spontaneously
hypothesis_generated: An explicit entropy-weighted training objective produces a critical metastable phase analogous to edge-of-chaos in standard LLMs

title: Dimensional Criticality at Grokking Across MLPs and Transformers
authors: Wang, P.
key_finding: VERIFY: TDU-OFC avalanche probe on MLP-XOR and transformer-modular-addition shows effective cascade dimension D(t) crosses the D=1 Gaussian baseline precisely at the grokking transition
hypothesis_generated: Direct scoop risk: cascade-dimension analysis of grokking is already published; our contribution must diverge in method (SAE-feature events) or system (pretrained LLMs)

title: Grokking as Dimensional Phase Transition in Neural Networks
authors: Wang, P.
key_finding: VERIFY: grokking is framed as a dimensional phase transition with sub- to super-diffusive crossover and self-organised criticality in gradient-field geometry
hypothesis_generated: Near-scoop companion to 3056: pre-register how our criticality signatures differ from Wang's gradient-geometry signatures

title: Intelligence at the Edge of Chaos
authors: Zhang, S.; Patel, A.; Rizvi, S.A.; Liu, N.; He, S.; Karbasi, A.; Zappala, E.; van Dijk, D.
key_finding: LLMs trained to predict elementary cellular-automaton dynamics acquire stronger downstream reasoning when the training CA sits at intermediate (edge-of-chaos) complexity than at trivial or fully chaotic complexity
hypothesis_generated: Complexity of training data itself carries a criticality sweet-spot — distinct from activation criticality but potentially couples to it

title: Sparse Autoencoders Can Interpret Randomly Initialized Transformers
authors: Heap, T.; Lawson, T.; Farnik, L.; Aitchison, L.
key_finding: VERIFY: SAE interpretability metrics are broadly similar on random-init and trained transformers — the user-requested 'Heimersheim & Turner' attribution does not match arXiv 2501.17727 authors
hypothesis_generated: Null-model warning: SAE-feature avalanches from random-init networks must be a control condition for any criticality claim

title: Lyapunov Learning at the Onset of Chaos
authors: Benati, M.; Falanti, A.; others
key_finding: VERIFY: training algorithm that regularises maximum Lyapunov exponent toward zero improves adaptation under regime shifts (Lorenz benchmark) by ~96% loss-ratio
hypothesis_generated: Explicit edge-of-chaos training objective provides an active-control baseline for our passive criticality measurements

title: Learning Continually by Spectral Regularization
authors: Lewandowski, A.; Tanaka, H.; Schuurmans, D.; Machado, M.C.
key_finding: VERIFY: keeping the top singular value of each layer near one preserves plasticity and prevents loss-of-trainability in continual learning
hypothesis_generated: Spectral-radius regularisation is effectively an edge-of-chaos constraint at weight level — compare to activation-level criticality metrics

title: GrokAlign: Geometric Characterisation and Acceleration of Grokking
authors: Walker, T.; Humayun, A.I.; Balestriero, R.; Baraniuk, R.G.
key_finding: Jacobian alignment at training points drives grokking; direct Jacobian regularisation induces grokking much earlier than weight decay alone
hypothesis_generated: Jacobian alignment is a geometric order parameter for grokking — test whether it co-varies with activation-criticality signatures

title: Grokfast: Accelerated Grokking by Amplifying Slow Gradients
authors: Lee, J.; Kang, B.G.; Kim, K.; Lee, K.M.
key_finding: Spectral decomposition of gradient trajectories shows slow components drive generalisation; amplifying them accelerates grokking >50x on image/language/graph tasks
hypothesis_generated: Slow-gradient amplification supplies a cheap lever for generating many grokking transitions to sample critical signatures

title: NeuralGrok: Accelerate Grokking by Neural Gradient Transformation
authors: Yu, X.; Zhang, Y.; others
key_finding: VERIFY: learned gradient-transformation module accelerates grokking on arithmetic tasks and reduces Absolute Gradient Entropy (AGE) more smoothly than weight decay
hypothesis_generated: Gradient-entropy is a candidate progress measure worth comparing against activation-avalanche measures

title: Muon Optimizer Accelerates Grokking
authors: Tveit, A.; Tidemann, A.; others
key_finding: VERIFY: Muon's spectral-norm constraints and second-order information cut mean grokking epoch from 153 to 103 across seven modular-arithmetic tasks (p=6.3e-8)
hypothesis_generated: Optimiser-level spectral constraint is another control knob for tuning where on the criticality axis grokking occurs

title: Grokking at the Edge of Numerical Stability
authors: Prieto, L.; Barsbey, M.; Mediano, P.A.M.; Birdal, T.
key_finding: Unregularised grokking drives logits to Softmax-Collapse and produces naive-loss-minimisation gradients; StableMax activation and orthogonal-gradient training each enable grokking without weight decay
hypothesis_generated: Softmax-Collapse is a concrete dynamical-instability boundary that may coincide with the activation-criticality transition

title: Grokking and Generalization Collapse: Insights from HTSR theory
authors: Prakash, H.K.; Martin, C.H.
key_finding: Training a 3-layer MLP on MNIST for 1e7 steps reveals a third anti-grokking phase detectable only by HTSR alpha<2 and Correlation-Trap outlier singular values
hypothesis_generated: HTSR weight-matrix exponents carry late-stage criticality signal not visible in activation statistics — a complementary axis

title: Mamba State-Space Models Are Lyapunov-Stable Learners
authors: Halloran, J.T.; Gulati, M.; Roysdon, P.F.
key_finding: Mamba/Mamba-2 SSM layers are provably Lyapunov-stable under mixed-precision and PEFT fine-tuning perturbations, unlike transformer baselines
hypothesis_generated: Architectural Lyapunov stability is antithetical to edge-of-chaos — test whether Mamba sits further from criticality than transformers on matched tasks

title: Transformers are SSMs: Generalized Models and Efficient Algorithms Through Structured State Space Duality
authors: Dao, T.; Gu, A.
key_finding: State-Space Duality framework connects SSMs and attention via structured semiseparable matrices and yields Mamba-2 with 2-8x speedup while matching transformer perplexity
hypothesis_generated: SSD framework lets us port transformer criticality tools to SSMs with shared mathematical substrate

title: The Shaped Transformer: Attention Models in the Infinite Depth-and-Width Limit
authors: Noci, L.; Li, C.; Li, M.B.; He, B.; Hofmann, T.; Maddison, C.J.; Roy, D.M.
key_finding: Softmax centred at identity plus width-dependent temperature yields a well-defined SDE limit in proportional infinite-depth-width regime, avoiding rank collapse
hypothesis_generated: Shaped-attention parametrisation is an explicit criticality-preserving design — benchmark it against unshaped nanoGPT

title: u-muP: The Unit-Scaled Maximal Update Parametrization
authors: Blake, C.; Eichenberg, C.; Dean, J.; Balles, L.; Prince, L.Y.; Deiseroth, B.; Cruz-Salinas, A.F.; Luschi, C.; Weinbach, S.; Orr, D.
key_finding: Combining muP with Unit Scaling gives activations/weights/gradients scale ~1 at init and enables stable FP8 training with simpler HP defaults across model sizes
hypothesis_generated: u-muP is a principled init that is scale-invariant by construction — an alternative null baseline to random Gaussian init

title: Geometric Dynamics of Signal Propagation Predict Trainability of Transformers
authors: Cowsik, A.; Nebabu, T.; Qi, X.-L.; Ganguli, S.
key_finding: Treating token representations as n interacting particles yields update equations that expose an order-chaos phase transition in transformer init hyperparameters
hypothesis_generated: Provides the explicit edge-of-chaos phase diagram in transformer init space that our empirical signatures should track

title: All you need is a good init
authors: Mishkin, D.; Matas, J.
key_finding: Layer-sequential unit-variance initialisation (orthonormal + per-layer variance normalisation) matches or beats bespoke deep-net init schemes on ImageNet/CIFAR
hypothesis_generated: Classical per-layer variance-normalised init as historical baseline for how criticality-aware init changes trainability

title: Fixup Initialization: Residual Learning Without Normalization
authors: Zhang, H.; Dauphin, Y.N.; Ma, T.
key_finding: Properly rescaled residual-branch init (Fixup) trains 10000-layer residual nets stably without any normalisation layer
hypothesis_generated: Normalisation-free deep residual training — useful control for isolating whether BN/LN interacts with criticality signatures

title: Critical Learning Periods in Deep Neural Networks
authors: Achille, A.; Rovere, M.; Soatto, S.
key_finding: Early-training sensory deficits cause irreversible performance loss in deep networks mirroring biological critical periods; Fisher-Information of weights tracks an information-plasticity window
hypothesis_generated: Network sensitivity to perturbation has an early critical window — test whether criticality onset aligns with this window

title: Critical Learning Periods Emerge Even in Deep Linear Networks
authors: Kleinman, M.; Achille, A.; Soatto, S.
key_finding: Fisher-Information-based critical-learning-period phenomenology appears even in deep linear networks, isolating it from nonlinearity
hypothesis_generated: Critical-period phenomenon is nonlinearity-independent — suggests criticality claims need linear-net controls

title: Edge of chaos as a guiding principle for modern neural network training
authors: Zhang, L.; others
key_finding: VERIFY: weight decay pushes a fully-analytical feedforward net toward the ordered phase; optimal lambda places it at the edge of chaos with peak Fashion-MNIST accuracy
hypothesis_generated: Weight-decay is directly an order-parameter knob — ablation sweep on weight decay should traverse critical point

title: Power Lines: Scaling Laws for Weight Decay and Batch Size in LLM Pre-training
authors: Bergsma, S.; Soboleva, D.; others
key_finding: VERIFY: optimal weight decay scales linearly with batch size and the AdamW timescale tau=B/(eta*lambda*D) follows a precise power law in tokens-per-parameter ratio
hypothesis_generated: Power-law scaling of optimal regularisation hints at underlying criticality — test whether criticality-optimal WD tracks Power-Lines prediction

title: The Unlocking Spell on Base LLMs: Rethinking Alignment via In-Context Learning
authors: Lin, B.Y.; Ravichander, A.; Lu, X.; Dziri, N.; Sclar, M.; Chandu, K.; Bhagavatula, C.; Choi, Y.
key_finding: Base vs aligned LLMs produce near-identical next-token distributions except on stylistic tokens; URIAL achieves alignment with three in-context examples
hypothesis_generated: Post-training alters a thin stylistic surface — criticality signatures should be similar base-vs-chat unless our measure captures deeper changes

title: LIMA: Less Is More for Alignment
authors: Zhou, C.; Liu, P.; Xu, P.; Iyer, S.; Sun, J.; Mao, Y.; Ma, X.; Efrat, A.; Yu, P.; Yu, L.; Zhang, S.; Ghosh, G.; Lewis, M.; Zettlemoyer, L.; Levy, O.
key_finding: Fine-tuning LLaMa-65B on 1000 curated prompts (no RLHF) matches or beats GPT-4/Bard/DaVinci003 in 43-65% of preference comparisons
hypothesis_generated: Instruction-following is cheap to install — reinforces that post-training is a shallow perturbation relative to pretraining criticality

title: Instance-dependent Early Stopping
authors: Yuan, S.; Lin, R.; Feng, L.; Han, B.; Liu, T.
key_finding: Per-instance stopping criterion halts training on already-mastered examples, reducing compute while preserving/improving test accuracy
hypothesis_generated: Per-instance training horizon varies — criticality signatures may be instance-dependent not just layer-dependent

title: Pythia: A Suite for Analyzing Large Language Models Across Training and Scaling
authors: Biderman, S.; Schoelkopf, H.; Anthony, Q.; Purohit, S.; Prashanth, U.S.; Raff, E.; Skowron, A.; Sutawika, L.; van der Wal, O.
key_finding: 16 LLMs from 70M to 12B params trained on identical data in identical order with 154 public checkpoints each, enabling controlled scaling/training-dynamics studies
hypothesis_generated: Pythia is the canonical corrected citation for Biderman et al. 2023 — fixes C7 cite bug in our draft

title: Mapping the Edge of Chaos: Fractal-Like Boundaries in The Trainability of Decoder-Only Transformer Models
authors: Torkamandi, B.
key_finding: Hyperparameter trainability boundary of decoder-only transformers exhibits self-similar fractal structure across scales in the Adam update iteration
hypothesis_generated: Trainability boundary is fractal — criticality measurements near it should exhibit scale-invariance signatures too

title: Critical attention scaling in long-context transformers
authors: Chen, S.; Lin, Z.; Polyanskiy, Y.; Rigollet, P.
key_finding: Attention exhibits a phase transition in scaling factor beta_n: insufficient collapses tokens to one direction, excessive reduces attention to identity; critical beta_n ~ log n justifies YaRN/Qwen scaling
hypothesis_generated: Attention scaling is an explicit criticality mechanism at the layer level — directly map beta_n to our activation-criticality measurements

title: Cognitive Activation and Chaotic Dynamics in Large Language Models: A Quasi-Lyapunov Analysis of Reasoning Mechanisms
authors: Li, X.; others
key_finding: VERIFY: per-layer Quasi-Lyapunov Exponent shows LLM information accumulation follows nonlinear exponential law with MLPs dominating final output; small input perturbations produce large reasoning-output divergence
hypothesis_generated: QLE is a direct per-layer criticality metric — compare to our avalanche-based per-layer metrics on same models

title: Evidence of Phase Transitions in Small Transformer-Based Language Models
authors: Hong, N.; Hong, T.
key_finding: KL divergence, vocabulary composition and word-length probes show synchronised discontinuities during training of small transformers, marking a random-to-coherent-word transition
hypothesis_generated: Lexical-level coherence order parameter complements our low-level criticality measures and should be tracked jointly

title: SpikeGPT: Generative Pre-trained Language Model with Spiking Neural Networks
authors: Zhu, R.-J.; Zhao, Q.; Li, G.; Eshraghian, J.K.
key_finding: RWKV-style spiking language model scales to 260M params; linear-time attention replacement and binary event-driven activations give 20x fewer ops on neuromorphic hardware
hypothesis_generated: Spiking LLM provides native spike-train substrate for direct avalanche analysis without SAE-feature-to-spike conversion

title: Spikformer: When Spiking Neural Network Meets Transformer
authors: Zhou, Z.; Zhu, Y.; He, C.; Wang, Y.; Yan, S.; Tian, Y.; Yuan, L.
key_finding: Spiking Self-Attention with spike-form Q/K/V and no softmax reaches 74.8% ImageNet top-1 with 4 timesteps and 66.3M params
hypothesis_generated: Transformer-shaped spiking architecture closes the gap with non-spiking baselines — viable control model for avalanche statistics

title: Training Spiking Neural Networks Using Lessons From Deep Learning
authors: Eshraghian, J.K.; Ward, M.; Neftci, E.O.; Wang, X.; Lenz, G.; Dwivedi, G.; Bennamoun, M.; Jeong, D.S.; Lu, W.D.
key_finding: snntorch framework + tutorials unify surrogate-gradient training of SNNs with modern DL tooling; arctan smoothing of Heaviside is default surrogate
hypothesis_generated: Reference software for generating biological-style spike trains from gradient-trained nets — toolchain for our spiking-model criticality experiments
